title: Forecasting Canadian Average Hourly Wages Using Time Series Models
excerpt:
Modeled monthly average hourly wages in Canada (Jan 1997–Nov 2024) using three 
forecasting frameworks — seasonal decomposition with an ARMA-fitted remainder, 
SARIMA (Box-Jenkins), and Holt-Winters exponential smoothing — training on 
pre-pandemic data (1997–2018) and evaluating on a 2019 holdout period via Mean 
Squared Prediction Error. The decomposition-based model was most accurate 
(MSPE = 0.0135), ahead of SARIMA (0.0321) and Holt-Winters (0.0327).
